response:
HackerRank provides several compliance and security resources that may help with your company's infosec process:

**GDPR and AI Law Compliance:**
- HackerRank has GDPR FAQs for HackerRank for Work available
- HackerRank Services and AI Laws FAQs are documented
- HackerRank's AI-powered plagiarism solutions have undergone an independent bias audit that is consistent with NYC AI Law

**Bias Audit Documentation:**
- Summary of Bias Audit Results for HackerRank's Image Analysis System
- Summary of Bias Audit Results of the HackerRank's Plagiarism Detection System for New York City's Local Law 144 (which received an overall "PASS" rating)

**Data Privacy Protections:**
- No personal data is shared in AI training
- Personally identifiable information (PII) is excluded from all training datasets
- Privacy protections are fully enforced in accordance with HackerRank's policies
- Your company's tests are never used for AI model testing or training

For specific form completion and additional compliance documentation tailored to your company's infosec requirements, a support agent will follow up. You can also contact support@hackerrank.com or reach out to your dedicated customer account manager if you have one.

Source: GDPR and NYC AI Laws, HackerRank Services and AI Laws FAQs, HackerRank AI Data Services FAQ, Summary of Bias Audit Results of the HackerRank's Plagiarism Detection System for New York City's Local Law 144
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus provides substantial compliance and security documentation including GDPR FAQs, AI law compliance, bias audit results, and data privacy protections that address the infosec process inquiry. Next: user can reference the documented compliance materials and contact support for specific form completion assistance.

response:
The Apply tab has been deprecated and is no longer available. To practice coding challenges, you should now use the Prepare tab instead. Here's how to access coding challenges:

1. Log in to HackerRank Community using your credentials
2. Go to the **Prepare** tab
3. Scroll to the **Prepare by Topics** section
4. Select a topic to practice available challenges (For example, Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Python)

The features that were previously in the Apply tab have been removed following its deprecation, but you can find all coding practice functionality under the Prepare tab.

Source: Where to find Home tab features, Introduction to Coding Challenges

For specific configuration of inactivity timeout durations on your account, a support agent can follow up to discuss ava

tool_calls.query: interview session timeout automatic end inactivity

response:
Based on the documentation, HackerRank interviews have a 60-minute timer that automatically ends the interview when it expires. The corpus doesn't specify different inactivity timeouts for candidates versus interviewers, or mention shorter inactivity periods like the 20-minute timeouts you're experiencing.

For screen sharing scenarios where interviewers are primarily watching the candidate's screen, you can use the Leave Interview and End Interview controls available under the "Leave" dropdown in the top right corner. The interview session will remain running and will not end unless an interviewer performs the "End Interview for all" action - it won't automatically end due to interviewer inactivity on their HackerRank screen.

You can also use the Virtual Lobby system to manage participants. Candidates can be moved to the lobby without ending the interview by clicking the down arrow beside the candidate's name and selecting "Move," then readmitted when needed.

For specific configuration of inactivity timeout durations on your account, a support agent can follow up to discuss available options.

Source: using virtual lobby in hackerrank interviews, coding mock interview
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus documents the 60-minute default interview-end timeout and Leave/End controls but no per-account inactivity knob. Next: try the Leave-Interview / lobby controls; for the specific timeout duration, a support agent will follow up.

verifier_claims.text: To remove an interviewer from the platform, you have two options depending on your specific needs:
**Option 1: Remove from Teams**
Click the **three-dot icon** next to the user's name and select **Remove from Teams**. This will remove them from teams
**Option 2: Remove Team Member (if they're part of a specific team)**
1. Locate the user in the list
2. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column
Admins can also perform bulk actions on multiple users, including adding or removing users from teams.

tool_calls.query: remove user from team member management

response:
To remove an interviewer from the platform, you have two options depending on your specific needs:

**Option 1: Remove from Teams**
Click the **three-dot icon** next to the user's name and select **Remove from Teams**. This will remove them from teams and transfer their resources to a new owner that you confirm.

**Option 2: Remove Team Member (if they're part of a specific team)**
If you're managing team members specifically:
1. Locate the user in the list
2. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column

Admins can also perform bulk actions on multiple users, including adding or removing users from teams.

Source: User Management, Removing a team member
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents user removal procedures through the three-dot menu 'Remove from Teams' option and team member deletion via the delete icon. Next: use the three-dot menu next to the user's name to select Remove from Teams or use the delete icon in the Action column for team member removal.

verifier_claims.text: The corpus only documents individual self-serve plan procedures for this; as a company account, you'll need an admin or
To pause your HackerRank subscription, follow these steps:
1. Click on the **profile icon** in the top-right corner and select **Settings** from the dropdown menu
2. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button - this will display a prompt about the **Pause Subscription** feature
4. Select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months)
Note that the Pause Subscription feature has specific prerequisites:
- You must have a **monthly subscription** for Individual Monthly - Basic or Interview Monthly plans
If your account doesn't meet these requirements or you need to cancel permanently instead of pausing, you can use the st

response:
The corpus only documents individual self-serve plan procedures for this; as a company account, you'll need an admin or account owner to perform these steps on your behalf.

To pause your HackerRank subscription, follow these steps:

1. Click on the **profile icon** in the top-right corner and select **Settings** from the dropdown menu
2. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button - this will display a prompt about the **Pause Subscription** feature
4. Select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months)

Note that the Pause Subscription feature has specific prerequisites:
- You must have an active subscription that started at least 30 days ago
- You must have a **monthly subscription** for Individual Monthly - Basic or Interview Monthly plans

If your account doesn't meet these requirements or you need to cancel permanently instead of pausing, you can use the standard cancellation process through **Manage Subscription** from the profile dropdown.

Source: Pause Subscription, Cancelling subscription
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents the pause subscription procedure and prerequisites, though it's focused on individual plans rather than company accounts. Next: company admin follows the pause subscription steps in Settings > Billing or contacts support if the account type doesn't qualify.

verifier_claims.text: You can update the name on your certificate. According to HackerRank's policy, you can update the name on your certifica
1. Open your certificate page
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display

The platform will update your certificate with the new name.

response:
You can update the name on your certificate. According to HackerRank's policy, you can update the name on your certificate only once per account, and when you update your name, the change applies to all your certificates. After you update it, you cannot change it again.

To update the name on your certificate:

1. Open your certificate page
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**

The platform will update your certificate with the new name.

Source: Certifications FAQs
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents the certificate name update procedure with step-by-step instructions and policy limitations. Next: user follows the four-step process to regenerate their certificate with the correct name.

verifier_claims.text: To stop Claude from crawling your website, you can block Anthropic's crawler by adding directives to your robots.txt fil
To block ClaudeBot from your entire website, add this to the robots.txt file in your top-level directory:
Please do this for every subdomain that you wish to opt out from.
If you want to limit crawling activity rather than block it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay extension:
Anthropic uses different robots for transparency and choice, and they respect anti-circumvention technologies and aim fo

response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you can block Anthropic's crawler by adding directives to your robots.txt file. Anthropic respects industry standard directives in robots.txt and honors "do not crawl" signals.

To block ClaudeBot from your entire website, add this to the robots.txt file in your top-level directory:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Please do this for every subdomain that you wish to opt out from.

If you want to limit crawling activity rather than block it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay extension:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Anthropic uses different robots for transparency and choice, and they respect anti-circumvention technologies and aim for minimal disruption by being thoughtful about crawling speed and respecting Crawl-delay directives.

Source: Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents how to block Anthropic's ClaudeBot crawler using robots.txt directives and explains their crawling principles. Next: user adds the specified robots.txt directives to block or limit crawling of their website.

verifier_claims.text: For Enterprise plans, you can set custom data retention periods with a minimum of 30 days. By default, data is retained
When you allow Claude to use your chats to help improve the models, several privacy protections are in place: your data
For specific information about how long your data will be used for model improvement purposes, you may need to contact s

response:
The corpus doesn't specify an exact duration for how long your data will be used for model improvement when you allow Claude to use your data. However, it provides information about data retention controls:

For Enterprise plans, you can set custom data retention periods with a minimum of 30 days. By default, data is retained indefinitely unless a custom retention period is set. Data retention is based on the last observed activity - for chats, the retention period starts from the time of the last message in the conversation.

When you allow Claude to use your chats to help improve the models, several privacy protections are in place: your data is automatically de-linked from your user ID before any review, access is limited to a small number of personnel involved in model training, and tools are used to filter or obfuscate sensitive data. Your data is used solely to make Claude better for everyone, and you maintain full control through your privacy settings.

For specific information about how long your data will be used for model improvement purposes, you may need to contact support for clarification on the model training data lifecycle.

Source: Configure custom data retention controls for Enterprise plans, I would like to input sensitive data into my chats with Claude. Who can view my conversations?
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus covers data retention controls and privacy protections for model improvement but doesn't specify exact duration for training data usage. Next: user can review privacy settings and contact support for specific model training data lifecycle details.

If you've confirmed regional availability and requests are still failing, contact AWS Support at https://aws.amazon.com/contact-us/ or reach out to your AWS account manager for Claude in Amazon Bedrock support inquiries. For community-based support, you can also visit AWS re:Post at https://repost.aws/.

Source: What AWS Regions are Claude models available in Amazon Bedrock?, I use Claude in Amazon Bedrock. Who do I contact for customer support inquiries?
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus provides regional availability check (most common cause of Bedrock failures) and AWS support contact info for persistent issues. Next: user checks region availability then contacts AWS Support if needed.

verifier_claims.text: To remove an employee who has left your company from your HackerRank hiring account:
1. Log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account using your credentials.
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.
4. Navigate to the **Users** tab to see all users in your account.
5. Locate the employee you want to remove.
6. Click the **three-dot icon** next to the user's name and select **Remove from Teams**.
Before removing the user, you may want to transfer ownership of any resources (like tests) they created to another team
1. In the **Users** tab, click the more options menu under the **Actions** column for that user.
2. Select **Transfer Ownership** to move their resources to another user.

Admins can also perform bulk actions on multiple users, including adding or removing users from teams if you need to man

response:
To remove an employee who has left your company from your HackerRank hiring account:

1. Log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account using your credentials.
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.
4. Navigate to the **Users** tab to see all users in your account.
5. Locate the employee you want to remove.
6. Click the **three-dot icon** next to the user's name and select **Remove from Teams**.

Before removing the user, you may want to transfer ownership of any resources (like tests) they created to another team member. To do this:
1. In the **Users** tab, click the more options menu under the **Actions** column for that user.
2. Select **Transfer Ownership** to move their resources to another user.
3. In the **Transfer Resource Ownership** dialog, specify the new owner and confirm the transfer.

Admins can also perform bulk actions on multiple users, including adding or removing users from teams if you need to manage several departing employees at once.

Source: 1508181842 teams management :: **User Management**, Transferring resource ownership, Managing team members
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents user removal procedures through Teams Management and includes resource transfer options for departing employees. Next: navigate to Teams Management > Users tab and use the three-dot menu to remove the employee from teams.

verifier_claims.text: To set up Claude LTI for your students, you'll need to work with your university's Claude for Education administrator, a
1. **Enable Claude LTI in Claude for Education** (requires Claude for Education administrator access):
2. **Install Claude LTI as an App in Canvas** (requires Canvas LMS administrator access):
Since you mentioned you're a professor wanting to set this up for your students, you'll likely need to contact your univ

response:
To set up Claude LTI for your students, you'll need to work with your university's Claude for Education administrator, as this requires administrative access to both Claude for Education and Canvas.

The setup process involves two main steps:

1. **Enable Claude LTI in Claude for Education** (requires Claude for Education administrator access):
   - Sign in as an administrator to Claude for Education
   - Navigate to Organization settings > Connectors
   - Find Canvas and click "Enable"
   - Input the required Canvas Domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID
   - Click "Save Changes"

2. **Install Claude LTI as an App in Canvas** (requires Canvas LMS administrator access):
   - In Canvas, go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps
   - Click "View App Configurations" then select "+ App"
   - Select Configuration Type "By Client ID"
   - Input the Client ID from your Canvas developer key
   - Click "Install" and refresh the course page

Since you mentioned you're a professor wanting to set this up for your students, you'll likely need to contact your university's administrator(s) who have the necessary administrative permissions for both Claude for Education and Canvas to complete this integration.

Source: Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus provides complete step-by-step LTI setup procedure for Canvas integration with Claude for Education, though it requires administrator access. Next: contact university administrators with Claude for Education and Canvas admin permissions to perform the integration steps.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend

evidence.title: support :: Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?
regulations fees :: The Visa system
visa rules
visa rules :: Visa Credit Card Rules & Regulations
regulations fees

verifier_claims.text: The merchant is correct. In the US Virgin Islands and other US territories (like Puerto Rico and Guam), merchants are pe

response:
The merchant is correct. In the US Virgin Islands and other US territories (like Puerto Rico and Guam), merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for credit card purchases. This is an exception to Visa's general rule that merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions. This exception applies only in the USA and US territories, and only for credit cards. If a merchant requires a minimum amount greater than US$10 on a credit card, or sets any minimum on a debit card, you should notify your Visa card issuer.

Source: Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?
justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly addresses minimum spend requirements in US Virgin Islands with specific $10 credit card exception policy. Next: user understands the legitimate $10 minimum and knows to contact card issuer if merchant exceeds this limit.
